title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Common Problems in Availing Life Insurance Policies Through Bima ASBA and How to Overcome Them
Purchasing life insurance is a significant choice for many. The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) launched Bima ASBA (Applications Supported by Blocked Amount) to ease premium payment. Policyholders can use this facility to authorise blocking premium amounts from their bank accounts through the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), where money is only debited upon policy approval.[1]
Though Bima ASBA has several benefits, candidates can face specific challenges. Let's examine the common problems experienced while applying for life insurance policies under Bima ASBA and the solutions to address them.
Top Challenges and Solutions
Limited Awareness and Understanding [1]
Challenge: Potential policyholders might not be aware of the Bima ASBA facility and can be unclear about its benefits and application procedure.
Solution: Take an active initiative to seek advice from your Insurance companies and financial advisors and make yourself aware of Bima ASBA. This can be done through guide brochures, workshops, and online content that detail the facility's features, advantages, and procedure-by-procedure application process. An informed customer is likely to utilise Bima ASBA optimally.
Technical Challenges with UPI Integration [2]
Challenge: Certain applicants can experience technical challenges in mapping their UPI accounts to the Bima ASBA system, resulting in transaction failures or delays.
Solution: To minimise technical problems, applicants must keep their UPI accounts active and ensure they have adequate transaction limits to cover the premium amount. Insurance companies can partner with banks and UPI service providers to provide smooth integration and diagnostic support for typical technical issues.
Inadequate Funds Causing Application Rejection [2]
Challenge: When the applicant's bank account does not have adequate funds to pay the premium amount while blocking, the Bima ASBA process cannot proceed, causing application delays or rejection.
Solution: Applicants can check their account balances before starting the Bima ASBA process to confirm that sufficient funds are held. Low-balance alerts can also be used to ensure adequate funds for premium payments. Insurers can have systems to inform applicants immediately if a transaction is declined due to low funds, enabling them to correct the situation quickly.
Delayed Policy Issuance and Fund Blocking [3][4]
Challenge: Life insurance policy underwriting can take a while. During this time, the premium amount is held up in the applicant's account, and they have limited access to their money.
Solution: IRDAI requires insurers to make policy issue decisions within 14 days of application receipt. If the insurer does not act within this period, the blocked amount automatically reverts to the applicant's account. Applicants should monitor such a provision and check their application status.
Lack of Transparency in Application Status [4]
Challenge: Applicants risk delays because they do not receive a timely update on their application's status, mainly when funds are blocked without good communication from the insurer.
Solution: Insurers can create clear communication channels so applicants can receive updates on their applications' progress. Periodic updates through SMS, email, or special customer portals can prevent anxiety and build trust.
Potential for Unauthorised Deductions [1]
Challenge: Applicants are concerned that unauthorised deductions are being made from their accounts during the Bima ASBA procedure.
Solution: Bima ASBA tightens security by blocking funds rather than deducting them upfront. This will pay premiums only upon policy issuance, thus safeguarding consumers' money if the policy is not issued. Customers must ensure they approve transactions via official and safe channels to avoid fraud.
Customer Reluctance Due to Traditional Preferences [1]
Challenge: Customers might prefer old premium payment methods and be unwilling to embrace the Bima ASBA facility because they are unfamiliar with or mistrust electronic payment systems.
Solution: Establishing customer trust is key to the adoption of Bima ASBA. Insurance companies can emphasise the security aspects, convenience, and advantages of Bima ASBA over conventional means. Demonstrations, word-of-mouth, and dispelling misconceptions can make customers adopt this new payment facility.
Conclusion [5]
Bima ASBA is a big leap in the ease of premium payments for life insurance policies in India. Granting the ability to block money instead of instant debiting gives policyholders better control and security over their finances. Yet, as with anything new, problems exist that can be addressed by insurers and applicants alike. With proper education, technology improvements, and open communication, these issues can be tackled so that Bima ASBA can live up to its full potential to revolutionise the Indian insurance payment scheme.
References:
https://www.outlookmoney.com/insurance/missed-premium-payments-how-bima-asba-will-prevent-lapsed-policies
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/industry/banking/finance/insure/bima-asba-facility-will-eliminate-unauthorised-deductions-say-insurance-sector-players/articleshow/118454586.cms
https://www.business-standard.com/finance/insurance/insurance-cos-knock-on-irdai-door-seeking-more-time-to-implement-bima-asba-125030900295_1.html
https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/personal-finance/how-policyholders-will-benefit-from-upi-linked-bima-asba-facility-for-premium-payments-12950693.html
https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/bima-asba-new-era-insurance-payments-policyx-com-i2aoc/

full_pdf_text:
Common Problems in Availing Life Insurance Policies Through Bima ASBA and How to Overcome Them
Purchasing life insurance is a significant choice for many. The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) launched Bima ASBA (Applications Supported by Blocked Amount) to facilitate smooth transactions of payment of premium . Applicants can authorise blocking premium amounts from their bank accounts through the Unified Payments Interface (UPI).
Premium amount will only be debited from the bank account upon policy approval.[1]
Though Bima ASBA has several benefits, candidates can face specific challenges. Let's examine the common problems that could be experienced while applying for life insurance policies under Bima ASBA and the solutions to address them. Top Challenges and Solutions Limited Awareness and Understanding [1] Challenge: Potential applicants might not be aware of the Bima ASBA facility and could be unclear about application procedure. Solution: Take an active initiative to seek advice from your Insurance companies and/or financial advisors and make yourself aware of Bima ASBA. You could also refer to online content that explain features of Bima ASBA in detail. Remember if you keep yourself well-informed you can opt where you wish to opt for Bima ASBA or traditional payment method. Technical Challenges with UPI Integration [2] Challenge: Certain applicants can experience technical challenges in mapping their UPI accounts to the Bima ASBA system, resulting in transaction failures or delays. Solution: To minimise technical problems, applicants must keep their UPI accounts active and ensure they have adequate transaction limits to cover the premium amount. Insurance companies can partner with banks and UPI service providers to provide smooth integration and diagnostic support for typical technical issues. Inadequate Funds Causing Application Rejection [2] Challenge: When the applicant's bank account does not have adequate funds to pay the premium amount while blocking, the Bima ASBA process cannot proceed, causing application delays or rejection. Solution: Applicants can check their account balances before starting the Bima ASBA process to confirm that sufficient funds are available. Low-balance alerts given by the Bank can also be referred to ensure adequate funds for premium payments. \ How To Use OTM (One Time Mandate)[3] Policyholders can simplify their premium payment process by following these steps:
1. Choose BIMA-ASBA – Select BIMA-ASBA as the payment method while purchasing the
policy.
2. Fund Blocking Request – The insurer, via its banking partner, requests to block the premium
amount. 3. Consent & Confirmation – The policyholder provides consent, and the bank notifies the
insurer once funds are blocked. 4. Funds on Hold – The blocked amount remains in the policy applicant’s account until policy
approval. But do note that you won’t be able to use these funds for any transactions till such time the underwriting decision is made by the Insurer. 5. Premium Payment – Upon approval, the insurer instructs the bank to release the funds. 6. Refund on Rejection – If the policy is cancelled or rejected, the full blocked amount is
released back to the policyholder within one working day. Conclusion [3] Bima ASBA is a big leap in the ease of premium payments for life insurance policies in India. Granting the ability to block money instead of instant debiting gives applicants better control and security over their finances. FAQs[4]
1. What is the UPI-linked Bima-ASBA facility?
o Bima-ASBA (Application Supported by Blocked Amount) is a payment method that allows policyholders to authorize the blocking of a specified premium amount in their bank account using UPI's One-Time Mandate (OTM). The premium is debited only after the insurer approves the policy. 2. How does Bima-ASBA benefit policyholders?
o Policyholders' funds remain in their accounts and continue to earn interest during the policy approval process. The premium amount is debited only upon policy issuance, ensuring better fund utilization and transparency. 3. What happens if my insurance application is rejected?
o If the insurer declines the policy application, the blocked funds are promptly released back into your account within one working day, eliminating the need for refund processing.
4. Is there a maximum limit for the premium amount under Bima-ASBA?
o Yes, the Bima-ASBA facility allows for the blocking of premium amounts up to ₹2 lakh using UPI's One-Time Mandate. 5. Which insurers currently offer the Bima-ASBA facility?
o Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance is among the first insurers to launch the Bima-ASBA facility. It's advisable to check with your insurer to confirm the availability of this payment option. 6. How does Bima-ASBA impact the interest earned on my bank account?
o Since the premium amount remains in your account until underwriting decision is made or 14 days whichever is earlier, and may continue to earn interest as per the extant legal provisions applicable to banks. 7. Can I use Bima-ASBA for all types of insurance policies?
o The availability of Bima-ASBA may vary depending on the insurer and the type of insurance policy. It's recommended to consult with your insurance provider to determine eligibility. 8. What are the steps to use the Bima-ASBA facility?
o To utilize Bima-ASBA, select it as your payment method when purchasing a policy. Authorize the blocking of the premium amount via UPI's One-Time Mandate. The funds will be blocked in your account and debited only upon policy approval. 9. Does Bima-ASBA ensure faster refunds?
o Yes, since the funds are only blocked and not debited until policy approval, any rejection results in immediate release of the blocked amount, facilitating quick
access to your funds. 10. Is Bima-ASBA similar to the ASBA facility used for IPO applications?
o Yes, Bima-ASBA is modeled after the ASBA facility used in IPO applications, where funds are blocked in the applicant's account and debited only upon allocation, ensuring efficient fund management. References:
1. https://www.outlookmoney.com/insurance/missed-premium-payments-how-bima-asba-
will-prevent-lapsed-policies 2. https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/industry/banking/finance/insure/bima-asba-facility-
will-eliminate-unauthorised-deductions-say-insurance-sector- players/articleshow/118454586.cms 3. https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/bima-asba-new-era-insurance-payments-policyx-com-
i2aoc/ 4. https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/personal-finance/how-applicants-will-
benefit-from-upi-linked-bima-asba-facility-for-premium-payments-12950693.html Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
